content: Notification table schema conventions: columns typically include id, user_id (recipient), type/action, source/related entity id+type, a rendered/templated message or structured payload, read boolean or read_at timestamp, created_at. A composite index on (user_id, is_read) or (user_id, read_at) is the standard index for fast unread-count queries; a composite index on (user_id, created_at DESC) supports paginated listing newest-first. At small-to-medium scale a live COUNT(*) WHERE user_id= AND read_at IS NULL against an indexed column is fine; denormalized counters are an optimization only needed at large scale.
